name: India Spiced Eggplant
recipeInstructions:
1. Place whole eggplant in a shallow pan and bake in an oven preheated to 400°F until very soft, about 50 minutes.
2. Alternatively, prick the eggplant with a fork, place it on paper towel, and microwave uncovered at 100% power for 18-20 minutes. Allow to cool slightly.
3. Cut the eggplant open and scoop out the pulp into large chunks, discarding larger seed pockets, stem, and skin. Coarsely chop the remaining pieces (some people prefer to leave the skin on and chop the eggplant into 1-inch cubes). Set aside in a colander to drain.
4. Coarsely slice the red onion and mince the ginger.
5. Heat the oil in a wide frying pan over medium heat. Add the cumin seeds, ginger, and sliced onion, and cook, stirring occasionally, until the onion is soft.
6. Coarsely chop the tomato and seeded green pepper, then add them to the onion mixture along with turmeric, coriander, ground cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes.
7. Add water and bring the mixture to a simmer. Reduce heat to low and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
8. Stir in the chopped eggplant and cook for an additional 5 minutes to heat through and blend flavors.
9. Just before serving, stir in the coarsely chopped cilantro.
Per serving: 2 g protein, 11 g carbohydrate, 0 g cholesterol, 80 calories.
